A leftover server process was listening on port `5012`, but it returned empty responses, so I stopped that process to leave things clean.

[2026-04-16 08:35:41] ASSISTANT

[2026-04-16 08:35:59] ASSISTANT
You are **partly done**, but not fully done yet according to the professor’s checklist.

**Checklist Status**

| Requirement | Status | Notes |
|---|---:|---|
| Use mock repository with static data from Lab 1 | **Done** | You have mock repositories for `Ekipa`, `Igrac`, `PrijavaEkipe`, `Stadion`, `Sudac`, `Turnir`, `Utakmica`. |
| Implement Index/list pages for each entity, without Create/Edit | **Partial** | Done for `Stadion`, `Ekipa`, `Sudac`, `Turnir`. Missing standalone pages for `Igrac`, `PrijavaEkipe`, and `Utakmica`. |
| Implement Details pages for each entity | **Partial** | Done for `Stadion`, `Ekipa`, `Sudac`, `Turnir`. Missing standalone details for `Igrac`, `PrijavaEkipe`, and `Utakmica`. |
| Implement specific page/custom home page or similar | **Not done** | `Home/Index.cshtml` is still the default ASP.NET “Welcome” page. This needs to become a custom page. |
| Complete navigation between all pages: menu, list-to-details links, breadcrumbs | **Partial** | Menu exists for `Stadioni`, `Ekipe`, `Sudci`, `Turniri`. List-to-details links exist for those. Breadcrumbs are not implemented, and missing entities are not in navigation. |

**What You Still Need**

You should still add:

1. **IgracController**
   - `/Igrac`
   - `/Igrac/Details/{id}`

2. **UtakmicaController**
   - `/Utakmica`
   - `/Utakmica/Details/{id}`
   - Should resolve teams, stadium, referee, tournament.

3. **PrijavaEkipeController**
   - `/PrijavaEkipe`
   - `/PrijavaEkipe/Details/{id}`
   - Should resolve team and tournament.

4. **Custom home page**
   - Replace the default `Welcome` page with something related to the football tournament system.
   - For example: dashboard cards linking to Turniri, Ekipe, Stadioni, Sudci, Utakmice, Igrači.

5. **Breadcrumbs**
   - Example on details pages:
   - `Home > Turniri > Ljetni nogometni turnir`
   - `Home > Ekipe > NK Zagreb`

**Important Note**

Your `Turnir` details page already shows a lot of related data, which is good. But the professor specifically says **“for each entity”**, so embedded data inside `Turnir/Details` probably does **not** fully replace having standalone Index and Details pages for `Igrac`, `Utakmica`, and `PrijavaEkipe`.

So: you are in a good spot, but I would not submit yet.

Best next step: implement `UtakmicaController` next, because it connects almost everything: tournament, teams, stadium, and referee.

## My request for Codex:
HOW do i configure new agent in the folder agents.

[2026-04-16 09:45:17] ASSISTANT
I’ll check the existing `.github/agents` files so I can explain the exact format your project is already using, not just give a generic answer.

[2026-04-16 09:45:18] TOOL
Command: Get-Content -Raw .github\agents\"Lab 2 - HTML Binding.md"
Exit Code: 1

[2026-04-16 09:45:47] ASSISTANT
To configure a new custom agent, you create a Markdown file in:

```text
.github/agents/
```

The file must end with:

```text
.agent.md
```

For example:

```text
.github/agents/UxExpert.agent.md
```

Your current `UxExpert.agent.md` file is empty, so Copilot/Codex will not have useful instructions from it yet.

A basic agent file should look like this:

```md
---
name: UxExpert
description: Reviews and improves UI/UX, navigation, layout, accessibility, and Razor page structure for this ASP.NET Core MVC project.
tools: ["read", "edit", "search"]
---

You are a UX expert for this football tournament organization system.

Focus on:
- Clear navigation between pages.
- Consistent page layout.
- Simple Bootstrap-based UI.
- Good labels for Croatian domain terms like Turniri, Ekipe, Igrači, Stadioni, Sudci, Utakmice, and Prijave.
- Making list pages easy to scan.
- Making details pages show the most important information first.
- Avoiding Create/Edit/Delete UI unless explicitly requested.

When reviewing or editing UI:
- Keep the app simple and suitable for a student MVC project.
- Use existing Bootstrap styles.
- Prefer readable tables for Index pages.
- Prefer `dl`, cards, or simple sections for Details pages.
- Make names clickable when they lead to details.
- Add breadcrumbs when useful.
```

The important parts are:

```yaml
---
name: UxExpert
description: ...
tools: [...]
---
```

Then below the frontmatter you write the actual behavior/instructions for the agent.

According to GitHub’s custom agent docs, agent profiles are Markdown files with YAML frontmatter, usually stored in `.github/agents`, and the `description` explains what the agent does. The `tools` property is optional; if omitted, the agent may have access to all available tools. Source: [GitHub Docs - Creating custom agents](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/how-tos/use-copilot-agents/coding-agent/create-custom-agents), [GitHub Docs - Custom agents configuration](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/reference/custom-agents-configuration).

For your case, you can put this directly into:

```text
.github/agents/UxExpert.agent.md
```

Then save it, commit it, and refresh/reopen the agent picker if your IDE does not show it immediately.

A good second agent for your project could be:

```text
.github/agents/MvcTutor.agent.md
```

Example:

```md
---
name: MvcTutor
description: Helps implement ASP.NET Core MVC controllers, Razor views, mock repositories, view models, routing, and navigation for the football tournament project.
tools: ["read", "edit", "search"]
---

You are an ASP.NET Core MVC tutor for this student project.

Help with:
- Controllers with Index and Details actions.
- Razor views using strongly typed models.
- Mock repositories with static data.
- Navigation links and breadcrumbs.
- Keeping code simple and beginner-friendly.

Rules:
- Do not add database code unless asked.
- Do not add Create/Edit/Delete unless asked.
- Prefer simple view models when a page needs related data.
- Keep UI consistent with existing Bootstrap layout.
```

So the quick answer is: **create or edit a `.agent.md` file inside `.github/agents`, add YAML frontmatter, then write the agent’s instructions below it.**

[2026-04-16 09:55:34] ASSISTANT
Done. I expanded [.github/agents/UxExpert.agent.md](</c:/Users/Mateo/source/repos/SustavZaOrganizacijuNogometnihTurnira/.github/agents/UxExpert.agent.md>) with detailed instructions for:

- vanilla CSS only
- no Bootstrap/Tailwind/UI libraries
- vanilla JavaScript or jQuery only
- efficient CSS organization
- suggested CSS file structure
- reusable CSS classes
- your preferred green/gold/neutral color scheme
- navigation and breadcrumb rules
- accessibility expectations
- MVC/Razor-specific UX guidance
- when to use view models instead of Razor lookup logic

I preserved your existing frontmatter and tools setup, then expanded the agent behavior below it.

One thing to keep in mind: your current app still uses Bootstrap from the default template. The agent instructions now say not to add/use UI libraries going forward, but if you want full consistency later, the next step would be removing Bootstrap references from `_Layout.cshtml` and replacing the Bootstrap classes with your own CSS.
